  • Orbital Maneuvers
  • API Reference
Back to top
View this page

API Reference¶

This section holds the formal documentation for ASTRA-Core’s underlying mathematical modules.

Maneuvers & Cowell Propagator¶

ASTRA Core Numerical Propagator — Segmented Cowell’s Method.

Implements a mission-operations–grade numerical orbit propagator using Cowell’s direct integration with a Dormand-Prince RK8(7) adaptive-step integrator.

Features:

  • 6-DOF Coast arcs: Two-body + J2/J3/J4 + drag + 3rd-body gravity.

  • 7-DOF Powered arcs: Adds attitude-steered thrust with Tsiolkovsky-coupled mass depletion.

  • Segmented Orchestrator: Automatically slices propagation at engine ignition/cutoff boundaries so the integrator never steps across a force-model discontinuity.

  • High-Fidelity Data Sources: - JPL DE421 Sun/Moon positions (via Skyfield, replacing analytical

    approximations).

    • Empirical atmospheric density parameterised by F10.7 solar flux and Ap geomagnetic index (replacing the static exponential model).

Force model includes:

  • Two-body Keplerian gravity

  • J2, J3, J4 zonal harmonic perturbations (WGS84)

  • Empirical atmospheric drag (Jacchia-class with space weather)

  • Solar third-body point-mass perturbation (JPL DE421)

  • Lunar third-body point-mass perturbation (JPL DE421)

  • Finite continuous thrust (7-DOF powered arcs)

References

Vallado, D. A. (2013). Fundamentals of Astrodynamics and Applications. Montenbruck & Gill (2000). Satellite Orbits. Park et al. (2021). JPL Planetary Ephemerides DE440/DE441.

class astra.propagator.DragConfig(cd: float = 2.2, area_m2: float = 10.0, mass_kg: float = 1000.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Atmospheric drag configuration for a specific object.

cd¶

Drag coefficient (dimensionless, typically 2.0–2.5).

Type:

float

area_m2¶

Cross-sectional area in m².

Type:

float

mass_kg¶

Object mass in kg.

Type:

float

area_m2: float = 10.0¶
cd: float = 2.2¶
mass_kg: float = 1000.0¶
class astra.propagator.NumericalState(t_jd: float, position_km: ndarray, velocity_km_s: ndarray, mass_kg: float | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Full kinematic state vector at a single epoch.

In 6-DOF (coast) mode, mass_kg is None and the state vector is [x, y, z, vx, vy, vz].

In 7-DOF (powered) mode, mass_kg tracks propellant depletion via Tsiolkovsky coupling: dm/dt = −F / (Isp·g₀).

t_jd¶

Julian Date of this state.

Type:

float

position_km¶

Shape (3,) ECI position [x, y, z] in km.

Type:

numpy.ndarray

velocity_km_s¶

Shape (3,) ECI velocity [vx, vy, vz] in km/s.

Type:

numpy.ndarray

mass_kg¶

Spacecraft wet mass in kg. None for coast-only runs.

Type:

float | None

mass_kg: float | None = None¶
position_km: ndarray¶
t_jd: float¶
velocity_km_s: ndarray¶
astra.propagator.propagate_cowell(state0: NumericalState, duration_s: float, dt_out: float = 60.0, drag_config: DragConfig | None = None, include_third_body: bool = True, rtol: float | None = None, atol: float | None = None, maneuvers: list[FiniteBurn] | None = None, use_de: bool = True, use_empirical_drag: bool = True, coast_rtol: float = 1e-08, coast_atol: float = 1e-08, powered_rtol: float = 1e-12, powered_atol: float = 1e-12) → list[NumericalState][source]¶

Propagate an orbit using segmented Cowell’s method with RK8(7).

This is a mission-operations–grade numerical propagator that automatically segments the integration timeline at engine ignition/cutoff boundaries. Each segment uses the appropriate derivative function:

  • Coast segments: 6-DOF [r, v] — gravitational + drag.

  • Powered segments: 7-DOF [r, v, m] — adds thrust and Tsiolkovsky mass depletion.

The segmented approach ensures that solve_ivp never steps across a force-model discontinuity, eliminating truncation error at burn edges.

Parameters:

  • state0 – Initial state (position + velocity + optional mass).

  • duration_s – Total propagation duration in seconds.

  • dt_out – Output time step in seconds (default 60 s).

  • drag_config – Optional atmospheric drag parameters.

  • include_third_body – Include Sun/Moon gravity.

  • rtol – Relative tolerance for adaptive step integrator.

  • atol – Absolute tolerance for adaptive step integrator.

  • maneuvers – Optional list of FiniteBurn definitions. Burns must not overlap in time.

  • use_de – Use JPL DE421 for Sun/Moon (True) or analytical (False).

  • use_empirical_drag – Use F10.7/Ap drag model (True) or static (False).

Returns:

List of NumericalState objects at each output time step. If maneuvers are present, each state includes the current mass.

TLE Parsing¶

ASTRA Core TLE parsing and validation.

This module is the entry point for all data entering ASTRA Core. It handles parsing, validation, and batch loading of Two-Line Element (TLE) sets from raw text.

astra.tle.load_tle_catalog(tle_lines: list[str]) → list[SatelliteTLE][source]¶

Parse a batch of TLE text lines into SatelliteTLE objects.

Invalid TLEs are skipped with a logged warning.

Parameters:

tle_lines – A flat list of strings, typically expected to be in triplets: name, line1, line2.

Returns:

List of successfully parsed SatelliteTLE objects.

Raises:

AstraError – If total parse failure occurs (result is empty but input was non-empty).

astra.tle.parse_tle(name: str, line1: str, line2: str) → SatelliteTLE[source]¶

Parse three raw TLE lines into a validated SatelliteTLE object.

Parameters:

  • name – Object name string.

  • line1 – TLE line 1.

  • line2 – TLE line 2.

Returns:

A fully populated SatelliteTLE instance.

Raises:

InvalidTLEError – If any validation fails.

astra.tle.validate_tle(name: str, line1: str, line2: str) → bool[source]¶

Non-destructive validation of TLE strings.

Parameters:

  • name – Object name string.

  • line1 – TLE line 1.

  • line2 – TLE line 2.

Returns:

True if TLE is well-formed and checksums pass, False otherwise.

Dataclasses & Models¶

ASTRA Core data models.

All persistent data structures used across the library are defined here as frozen dataclasses. This is the single source of truth for all inter-module data types — no other module defines dataclasses.

Every dataclass uses frozen=True to enforce immutability.

class astra.models.ConjunctionEvent(object_a_id: str, object_b_id: str, tca_jd: float, miss_distance_km: float, relative_velocity_km_s: float, collision_probability: float, risk_level: str, position_a_km: ndarray, position_b_km: ndarray)[source]¶

Bases: object

Detected close-approach event between two orbital objects.

Primary output of conjunction analysis.

object_a_id¶

NORAD ID of first object (lexicographically smaller).

Type:

str

object_b_id¶

NORAD ID of second object (lexicographically larger).

Type:

str

tca_jd¶

Time of Closest Approach as Julian Date.

Type:

float

miss_distance_km¶

Minimum separation distance at TCA (km).

Type:

float

relative_velocity_km_s¶

Relative speed at TCA (km/s).

Type:

float

collision_probability¶

Chan-method probability (0.0–1.0).

Type:

float

risk_level¶

"LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH", or "CRITICAL".

Type:

str

position_a_km¶

Shape (3,) TEME position of object A at TCA (km).

Type:

numpy.ndarray

position_b_km¶

Shape (3,) TEME position of object B at TCA (km).

Type:

numpy.ndarray

collision_probability: float¶
miss_distance_km: float¶
object_a_id: str¶
object_b_id: str¶
position_a_km: ndarray¶
position_b_km: ndarray¶
relative_velocity_km_s: float¶
risk_level: str¶
tca_jd: float¶
class astra.models.DebrisObject(tle: SatelliteTLE, altitude_km: float, inclination_deg: float, period_minutes: float, raan_deg: float, eccentricity: float, apogee_km: float, perigee_km: float, object_class: str, rcs_m2: float | None = None, radius_m: float | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Cataloged orbital object with pre-derived parameters for filtering.

Contains the authoritative SatelliteTLE plus derived orbital metrics that enable fast filtering without propagation.

tle¶

Authoritative TLE source.

Type:

astra.models.SatelliteTLE

altitude_km¶

Mean orbital altitude above Earth’s surface (km).

Type:

float

inclination_deg¶

Orbital inclination in degrees (0–180).

Type:

float

period_minutes¶

Orbital period in minutes.

Type:

float

raan_deg¶

Right Ascension of the Ascending Node (0–360 degrees).

Type:

float

eccentricity¶

Orbital eccentricity (0.0 ≤ e < 1.0).

Type:

float

apogee_km¶

Apogee altitude (km above surface).

Type:

float

perigee_km¶

Perigee altitude (km above surface).

Type:

float

object_class¶

Same as tle.object_type.

Type:

str

altitude_km: float¶
apogee_km: float¶
eccentricity: float¶
inclination_deg: float¶
object_class: str¶
perigee_km: float¶
period_minutes: float¶
raan_deg: float¶
radius_m: float | None = None¶
rcs_m2: float | None = None¶
tle: SatelliteTLE¶
class astra.models.FilterConfig(min_altitude_km: float | None = None, max_altitude_km: float | None = None, lat_min_deg: float | None = None, lat_max_deg: float | None = None, lon_min_deg: float | None = None, lon_max_deg: float | None = None, t_start_jd: float | None = None, t_end_jd: float | None = None, object_types: tuple[str, ...] | None = None, max_objects: int | None = None)[source]¶

Bases: object

Filter parameters for the multi-stage debris filtering pipeline.

Passed as a single configuration object to apply_filters(). None fields are treated as “no constraint” for that dimension.

min_altitude_km¶

Lower altitude bound (km), inclusive.

Type:

float | None

max_altitude_km¶

Upper altitude bound (km), inclusive.

Type:

float | None

lat_min_deg¶

Minimum latitude of geographic region (deg).

Type:

float | None

lat_max_deg¶

Maximum latitude of geographic region (deg).

Type:

float | None

lon_min_deg¶

Minimum longitude of geographic region (deg).

Type:

float | None

lon_max_deg¶

Maximum longitude of geographic region (deg).

Type:

float | None

t_start_jd¶

Analysis window start as Julian Date.

Type:

float | None

t_end_jd¶

Analysis window end as Julian Date.

Type:

float | None

object_types¶

Tuple of allowed object types, e.g. ("DEBRIS", "ROCKET_BODY").

Type:

tuple[str, …] | None

max_objects¶

Hard cap on the number of survivors.

Type:

int | None

lat_max_deg: float | None = None¶
lat_min_deg: float | None = None¶
lon_max_deg: float | None = None¶
lon_min_deg: float | None = None¶
max_altitude_km: float | None = None¶
max_objects: int | None = None¶
min_altitude_km: float | None = None¶
object_types: tuple[str, ...] | None = None¶
t_end_jd: float | None = None¶
t_start_jd: float | None = None¶
class astra.models.FiniteBurn(epoch_ignition_jd: float, duration_s: float, thrust_N: float, isp_s: float, direction: tuple[float, float, float], frame: ManeuverFrame)[source]¶

Bases: object

Finite-duration thrust maneuver for the 7-DOF Cowell propagator.

Models a continuous engine burn with dynamically steered thrust (re-computed at every integration sub-step) and Tsiolkovsky-coupled mass depletion.

All epochs are Julian Dates. Thrust direction is given as a unit vector in the chosen ManeuverFrame; the engine magnitude is set by thrust_N.

epoch_ignition_jd¶

Julian Date of engine ignition.

Type:

float

duration_s¶

Burn duration in seconds (> 0).

Type:

float

thrust_N¶

Engine thrust magnitude in Newtons (> 0).

Type:

float

isp_s¶

Specific impulse in seconds (> 0).

Type:

float

direction¶

Unit-length thrust direction in frame. Shape (3,), e.g. [1, 0, 0] for a pure V-axis burn in VNB.

Type:

tuple[float, float, float]

frame¶

Reference frame of direction.

Type:

astra.models.ManeuverFrame

direction: tuple[float, float, float]¶
duration_s: float¶
property epoch_cutoff_jd: float¶

Julian Date of engine cutoff.

epoch_ignition_jd: float¶
frame: ManeuverFrame¶
isp_s: float¶
property mass_flow_rate_kg_s: float¶

Propellant mass flow rate dm/dt (kg/s, positive value).

Derived from the Tsiolkovsky relation:

dm/dt = F / (Isp * g₀)

thrust_N: float¶
class astra.models.ManeuverFrame(*values)[source]¶

Bases: Enum

Reference frame for specifying maneuver thrust direction.

VNB (Velocity-Normal-Binormal):

  • V: Along the instantaneous velocity vector.

  • N: Along the orbital angular momentum (R × V).

  • B: Completes the right-handed triad (V × N).

Preferred for orbit-raising and lowering burns because the thrust is aligned exactly with the velocity vector, even on eccentric orbits.

RTN (Radial-Transverse-Normal) / RIC (Radial-Intrack-Crosstrack):

  • R: Along the geocentric radial (position) vector.

  • T: Perpendicular to R in the orbital plane, roughly along-track.

  • N: Along the orbital angular momentum (R × V).

Preferred for station-keeping and relative navigation.

RTN = 'RTN'¶
VNB = 'VNB'¶
class astra.models.Observer(name: str, latitude_deg: float, longitude_deg: float, elevation_m: float, min_elevation_deg: float = 10.0)[source]¶

Bases: object

Ground-based observation station.

Required by all visibility functions.

name¶

Human-readable station name.

Type:

str

latitude_deg¶

WGS84 geodetic latitude (-90 to +90).

Type:

float

longitude_deg¶

WGS84 longitude (-180 to +180).

Type:

float

elevation_m¶

Station elevation above MSL in metres.

Type:

float

min_elevation_deg¶

Minimum elevation angle to consider a pass visible (default 10°).

Type:

float

elevation_m: float¶
latitude_deg: float¶
longitude_deg: float¶
min_elevation_deg: float = 10.0¶
name: str¶
class astra.models.OrbitalState(norad_id: str, t_jd: float, position_km: ndarray, velocity_km_s: ndarray, error_code: int)[source]¶

Bases: object

Complete kinematic state of a single object at one instant.

Produced by SGP4 propagation for a single satellite at a single time step.

norad_id¶

Identifier linking back to the source SatelliteTLE.

Type:

str

t_jd¶

Julian Date of this state.

Type:

float

position_km¶

Shape (3,) array [x, y, z] in TEME, km.

Type:

numpy.ndarray

velocity_km_s¶

Shape (3,) array [vx, vy, vz] in TEME, km/s.

Type:

numpy.ndarray

error_code¶

SGP4 error code (0 = success, 1–6 = failure mode).

Type:

int

error_code: int¶
norad_id: str¶
position_km: ndarray¶
t_jd: float¶
velocity_km_s: ndarray¶
class astra.models.PassEvent(norad_id: str, observer_name: str, aos_jd: float, tca_jd: float, los_jd: float, max_elevation_deg: float, azimuth_at_aos_deg: float, azimuth_at_los_deg: float, duration_seconds: float)[source]¶

Bases: object

Satellite pass over a ground observer.

A time interval during which the satellite’s elevation angle exceeds the observer’s minimum elevation threshold.

norad_id¶

NORAD ID of the satellite.

Type:

str

observer_name¶

Name of the ground observer.

Type:

str

aos_jd¶

Acquisition of Signal — rise above min elevation (JD).

Type:

float

tca_jd¶

Time of max elevation (JD).

Type:

float

los_jd¶

Loss of Signal — drops below min elevation (JD).

Type:

float

max_elevation_deg¶

Maximum elevation reached during pass.

Type:

float

azimuth_at_aos_deg¶

Azimuth angle at AOS.

Type:

float

azimuth_at_los_deg¶

Azimuth angle at LOS.

Type:

float

duration_seconds¶

Total pass duration (los_jd - aos_jd) * 86400.

Type:

float

aos_jd: float¶
azimuth_at_aos_deg: float¶
azimuth_at_los_deg: float¶
duration_seconds: float¶
los_jd: float¶
max_elevation_deg: float¶
norad_id: str¶
observer_name: str¶
tca_jd: float¶
class astra.models.SatelliteTLE(norad_id: str, name: str, line1: str, line2: str, epoch_jd: float, object_type: str, rcs_m2: float | None = None, radius_m: float | None = None, dimensions_m: tuple[float, float, float] | None = None, attitude_quaternion: tuple[float, float, float, float] | None = None, attitude_mode: str = 'TUMBLING')[source]¶

Bases: object

Parsed Two-Line Element set for a single orbital object.

This is the entry point for all computations. Any satellite, debris, or rocket body that can be analysed in ASTRA Core must first exist as a SatelliteTLE.

norad_id¶

NORAD Catalog Number (1–9 digits, numeric-only string).

Type:

str

name¶

Object name (up to 24 characters, may contain spaces).

Type:

str

line1¶

Raw TLE Line 1 (exactly 69 characters, no newline).

Type:

str

line2¶

Raw TLE Line 2 (exactly 69 characters, no newline).

Type:

str

epoch_jd¶

TLE epoch as Julian Date.

Type:

float

object_type¶

One of "PAYLOAD", "ROCKET_BODY", "DEBRIS", "UNKNOWN".

Type:

str

attitude_mode: str = 'TUMBLING'¶
attitude_quaternion: tuple[float, float, float, float] | None = None¶
dimensions_m: tuple[float, float, float] | None = None¶
epoch_jd: float¶
classmethod from_strings(line1: str, line2: str, name: str = 'Unknown') → SatelliteTLE[source]¶

Create a SatelliteTLE directly from two raw lines, auto-calculating epoch.

Parameters:

  • line1 – Valid TLE line 1.

  • line2 – Valid TLE line 2.

  • name – Optional name for the satellite.

Returns:

A fully initialized SatelliteTLE object.

line1: str¶
line2: str¶
name: str¶
norad_id: str¶
object_type: str¶
radius_m: float | None = None¶
rcs_m2: float | None = None¶
astra.models.projected_area_m2(dimensions_m: tuple[float, float, float], attitude_quaternion: tuple[float, float, float, float], rel_vel_direction: ndarray) → float[source]¶

Compute projected cross-sectional area of a box onto the B-plane.

Models the satellite as a rectangular cuboid with given dimensions, rotated by the attitude quaternion. Projects the 3 face normals onto the relative velocity direction and sums visible face areas.

This replaces the isotropic sphere assumption for collision geometry.

Parameters:

  • dimensions_m – (length, width, height) in meters.

  • attitude_quaternion – (w, x, y, z) unit quaternion.

  • rel_vel_direction – (3,) unit vector along relative velocity.

Returns:

Projected area in m².

Conjunction Analysis¶

ASTRA Core conjunction detection module.

Detects close-approach events between pairs of orbital objects. Implements an advanced 3-phase optimization algorithm including: 1. Sweep-and-Prune Radial Bounding Shell filter 2. Trajectory AABB volume filter 3. Cubicpline Curvilinear Interpolation for exact sub-second TCA 4. Realistic Encounter-Plane Covariance Probability

astra.conjunction.closest_approach(trajectory_a: ndarray, trajectory_b: ndarray, times_jd: ndarray) → tuple[float, float, int][source]¶

Find the exact minimum separation using cubic splines.

astra.conjunction.distance_3d(pos_a: ndarray, pos_b: ndarray) → ndarray[source]¶

Compute Euclidean distance between two position arrays.

astra.conjunction.find_conjunctions(trajectories: dict[str, ndarray], times_jd: ndarray, elements_map: dict[str, DebrisObject], threshold_km: float = 5.0, coarse_threshold_km: float = 50.0) → list[ConjunctionEvent][source]¶

Find highly precise conjunction events using cubic spline interpolation.
